objective: Correct the Helixion/JOC rebuild from a large vertical panel board into a JOC-grade maintained work surface with single active pages, stable shell zones, page-local drawers, universal inspector drawers, and bottom timeline/event rail.

sections.INTENT: Rebuild Helixion/JOC/dAimon/WisdomNET as a usable operational cockpit, not a monolithic dashboard. The operator should reach Docs/Packages, Codex Chat, queue, extension, DOM perception, gates, receipts, and WisdomNET through top-level pages and special drawers, while internal ION machinery is translated into route, status, proof, memory, timeline, and next-step surfaces.
CORRECTIVE_FINDING: Recent React panels created useful projection inventory but repeated the known monolith failure by placing many large panels in one vertical main surface.

The current product surface used by the operator is http://127.0.0.1:8765/cockpit/chat, while the new React shell is on http://127.0.0.1:8788/helixion/development; this split is confusing until the shell route and product hierarchy are settled.
No further broad UI implementation should proceed until the page/drawer/timeline architecture below is used as the implementation contract.

SHELL_ZONES.purpose: Primary pages, mission/status chips, project/version sync, service health, and critical gate indicators. It must include direct access to Chat, JOC, Queue, Codex, Extension, Docs/Packages, Projects, WisdomNET, Gates, Receipts, and Settings without overlapping content.
Stable icon rail for active page instruments. It opens page-local drawers and never becomes the global navigation replacement.
Page-local controls, filters, favorites, tree selectors, model/context lenses, calibration tools, and page-specific actions. It uses tabs when content would otherwise scroll heavily.
Exactly one active page body at a time: Chat, Mission/JOC, Queue, Codex, Extension, Docs/Packages, Projects, WisdomNET, Gates, Receipts, or Settings. Only the active page owns substantial scroll.

Universal assistant/context/evidence/user/settings drawer. On non-chat pages it shows compact Codex/dAimon chat and selected object details. On chat page it shows selected-turn context, route, receipts, and proof.
Universal inspector mode selector: Assistant, Context, Evidence, Receipts, Health, Settings.
Temporal truth rail for queue events, model calls, file/package operations, DOM capture, receipts, service state, gates, and user corrections. It is filterable by event lane.

sections.USER_PRIMARY_FLOW: The operator opens the existing cockpit, chooses a top-level page such as Docs/Packages, sees a focused main surface, opens page-local drawers for favorites/tree/actions, uses the right inspector for detail/chat/proof, and watches the bottom timeline for what the system is doing. The operator should not hunt through a vertical wall of internal panels.

DRAWER_AND_LENS_MATRIX.main_surface: thumbnail/favorites grid plus selected package/document detail
ION assembled context packages and current project cards
portable dAimon companion, DOM perception, capture/calibration, and browser-carrier health
queue packets, autoplay/manual control, output-stopped detector, and gateway receipts

SCREEN_AND_INTERACTION_STATES.user_feedback: Show which page/view-model/service is loading, with skeleton regions inside the correct shell zone.
Show elapsed wait, active detector state, active packet/run, and safe pause/stop controls in the relevant page and bottom timeline.
Name the failing service/data source and offer inspect/retry/fix buttons when a local service repair path exists.
Show purposeful empty states: no selected package, no queue packets, no DOM capture, no active project, with the next lawful selection visible.
Show blocked authority as a clear gate: no production, no silent browser send, no unrestricted browser control, no package acceptance without receipts.
For future extension runtime only, show zip percentage, selected drop target, approval state, and cancellation path.

COMPONENT_CONTRACTS.testable_obligation: Only one main page renders as active; top bar changes pages without creating a vertical dashboard.
Docs/Packages and JOC development surfaces are reachable from the operator's current cockpit without unclear port jumping.
Drawer content is page-specific and tabbed before it becomes scroll-heavy.
Selected object details, assistant chat, context, and proof do not compete as main-page panels.
Async work, receipts, gates, and corrections are visible as time-ordered events with filters.
Shows thumbnails/favorites as a real page and sends detail/proof/actions to drawers/inspector, not a long panel stack.

CONTEXT_AND_PROOF_VISUALIZATION: Context packages, Capsule, Mini, HOT_CONTEXT, route refs, and package selections appear as user-facing context/proof lenses.
Receipts, validation gaps, and non-claims remain visible in right inspector and bottom timeline.
Do not display raw hidden reasoning, secrets, or unread files as if read.
Current panel inventory from C-079 through C-083 may be reused as data surfaces only after mapping each surface to a page/drawer/inspector/timeline zone.
MISSION_SESSION_DRIVER_SURFACES: local service health and repair state
Codex Capsule Chat response carrier state
INTELLIGENCE_MAP_OR_CONTEXT_SELECTION: why the context package is selected
DOM capture evidence and selector/geometry proof
VALIDATION_PLAN: No visual completion claim until screenshot/browser validation is explicitly run.
Build validation may be run only when implementation resumes.
Add shell-zone structure checks for top bar, left rail/drawer, main page, right inspector/rail, and bottom timeline.
Add no-monolith regression: docs/packages and queue pages must not render as one continuous wall of panels.
Add interaction checks for page switch, drawer switch, right inspector switch, timeline filter, and Docs/Packages top-bar access.
NON_CLAIMS: This packet is candidate current-context work, not accepted canon.
It does not claim the current React prototype is visually correct.
It does not claim the current 8765 cockpit and 8788 JOC shell are product-integrated.
It does not authorize live browser control, silent send, production mutation, or package acceptance.
It does not claim tests, screenshots, or browser validation were run.
sections.SETTLEMENT_ROUTE: JOC_UI_CANON_STEWARD reviews source alignment, FRONTEND_WORK_SURFACE_ARCHITECT owns the shell/page/drawer contract, INTERACTION_STATE_WEAVER covers loading/slow/error/permission states, CONTEXT_VISUALIZATION_CARTOGRAPHER covers context/proof lenses, COMPONENT_BUILDER implements bounded patches, and VISUAL_PROOF_AUDITOR checks build/screenshot/layout evidence before any done claim.

next_packet.objective: Refactor the local JOC prototype from vertical panel board into the shell/page/drawer architecture defined above, then decide whether it belongs inside the 8765 Codex Chat cockpit, the 8788 local JOC service, or a unified route.
next_packet.implementation_rule: No new feature panels until each existing surface is mapped to a shell zone.

expected_failure_kinds: current prototype still needs visual proof
current implementation history includes monolith-risk panels
